config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena itu mudah disalahgunakan oleh penguasa atau mayoritas. Pengalaman di Indonesia menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai tanpa batas yang jelas, hak warga kecil sering terabaikan. Bagi saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat selalu di atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that kind of idea has too often been used to excuse overreach by the government or the majority. Public safety, health, and basic order matter, but individual rights are exactly what protect people when “the common good” becomes a convenient slogan for abuse. I would only put society first in narrow, clearly justified cases, not as a general rule. The default should be that the burden is on society to prove why a restriction on individual rights is necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, menggusur, atau membungkam kritik, jadi kalau dibuat sebagai prinsip umum itu berbahaya. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti bencana, wabah, atau keamanan publik yang menuntut pembatasan sementara, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, jelas, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan permanen untuk mengorbankan hak orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat kuat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi, bukan ketika individu selalu diminta mengalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, that principle has too often been a cover for government overreach, mob pressure, or vague “public interest” claims that end up hurting unpopular people first. I do think there are limited exceptions for emergencies like epidemics or immediate threats to public safety, but those should be narrow, temporary, and tightly justified. If we make “society first” the default, individual rights stop being real protections and become privileges the majority can revoke.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya besar ketika frasa “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu mudah untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembungkaman kritik, atau kebijakan yang merugikan warga kecil. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana atau wabah yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan sementara, tetapi itu harus sangat ketat, transparan, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan alasan permanen. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu, bukan dari kebiasaan meminta orang selalu mengalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should generally outrank the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, I am wary of any broad “society first” principle because it has repeatedly been used to justify government overreach, majority pressure, and the mistreatment of unpopular people. That said, I do think narrow emergencies like epidemics or immediate threats can justify temporary limits, but only with clear standards and real accountability. If society’s interests always come first, then individual rights stop functioning as protections and become something the powerful can override whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
